sourceMovie.title: The Peasants
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Peasants (2023) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Hugh Welchman's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Anna Karenina
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Peasants clicked for you, Anna Karenina (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in history and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Peasants clicked for you, The House (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Peasants?
faq.answer: Try Anna Karenina, The House, The Bone Collector—each shares DNA with The Peasants while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Peasants?
faq.answer: The Peasants (2023) is best described as Animation, History, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Peasants worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
